w:r.w:t: The anti-streaming-app for people who actually care about cinema.

CineSins is a next-generation movie discovery and review platform built for audiences who are tired of generic recommendation engines and shallow ratings. Unlike Letterboxd, IMDb, or Rotten Tomatoes, CineSins inverts the review paradigm — movies are scored by their flaws, explored through cinematic DNA, and matched to viewers via mood-driven intelligence.

Built on Next.js 14, CineSins combines the social energy of Reddit, the cinematic soul of a film journal, and the chaos of a debate club into one cohesive product. It is designed from scratch to be AI-augmentable, scalable, and viral.
To become the internet's most opinionated, honest, and entertaining place to discover and dissect cinema.
IMDb and Rotten Tomatoes reduce film to a number — context and nuance are lost.
Netflix's recommendation engine optimizes for watch-time, not taste development.
Letterboxd is great for logging but has no interactive or debate-driven social layer.
No platform addresses the 'what to watch right now' paralysis with real empathy.
User reviews are unstructured noise with no accountability or competitive dimension.
Watches 5+ films/week, strong opinions, wants to argue
Watches on weekends, overwhelmed by choice

For film lovers who are bored of stars and algorithms — CineSins is the only movie platform that rewards honest criticism, fuels debate, and finds your next film through the lens of your current emotional state.

Instead of 1–5 stars, users assign a SIN COUNT to a movie — a number representing how many flaws, plot holes, lazy tropes, or bad decisions exist. Lower sins = better film. The brand's signature mechanic.
Sin counter input (0–999) on every movie detail page
Sins broken down into categories: Plot Sins, Acting Sins, Logic Sins, Tone Sins, Technical Sins
Aggregate Sin Score displayed as a public number with badge tiers: 'Sinless', 'Minor Offender', 'Career Criminal', 'Unforgivable'
Users can leave a one-line 'Sin Note' per category
Sin Score history graph showing how score evolved over community votes
'Sin Sentence' auto-generated: a summary line from the top-voted sin notes
Store sin votes in Supabase with user_id, movie_id, category, count, note
Aggregate with realtime Supabase subscriptions for live updates
Badge tiers computed server-side via Next.js API routes

A brutalist questionnaire that asks 5 questions about the user's current emotional and social state, then returns ONE single movie recommendation — not a list. Solves decision paralysis with personality.
5-question flow: Emotional state / Social context / Energy level / Darkness tolerance / Era preference
Each question uses unconventional, honest language ('Are you emotionally fragile right now?')
Result page: full-screen movie poster, Sin Score, one-paragraph Oracle verdict
'Not feeling it' button to regenerate once without re-taking the quiz
Shareable Oracle result card (OG image generated via Next.js API route)
Oracle history stored per user — track your mood vs. watch patterns over time
Matching algorithm: weighted cosine similarity on mood tags attached to each film
Mood tags curated via OMDb genre + community tagging in Phase 2
Shareable card generated with @vercel/og or satori
Each film gets a visual 'DNA strip' — a horizontal bar of colored blocks representing pacing, tone shifts, and emotional arc derived from community data. Like an EKG for a movie.
DNA strip rendered as SVG with 20 segments across film runtime
Each segment has: Pacing (slow/fast), Tone (dark/light/tense/comedic), Emotion (calm/anxious/joyful/grief)
Community-voted per segment in Phase 2 — seeded from genre/plot tags in Phase 1
Hover/tap reveals segment tooltip: timestamp + community description
DNA comparison view: place two films side-by-side
Show users which films they should avoid based on their watch history. The inverse recommendation — darkly funny, brutally honest, and uniquely shareable.
'Enter The Void' CTA on user profile page
Algorithm computes bottom 5 worst-matched films from unwatched catalog
Each Void entry shows: Film title, Sin Score, why it's wrong for you (generated reason)
'Dare Mode' — watch a Void film and report back, earns special badge
Shareable 'My Void' card listing your top 3 films to avoid
Your personal review is not a paragraph — it's a scene-by-scene journal. Users timestamp emotional reactions while watching. Creates a cinematic diary, not a text dump.
Review editor with timestamp input (HH:MM:SS) + reaction text + emotion tag
Journal view: timeline-style display sorted by timestamp
Final verdict section: overall Sin Score + Director's Note (unlimited text)
Other users can 'echo' a specific timestamp reaction (agree button)
Most-echoed timestamp becomes the movie's 'Key Moment' on the detail page
Structured async debates on any movie. Two users make formal arguments — Claim → Counter → Verdict voted by community. Like a courtroom for cinema.
Any logged-in user can open a Debate with a 'Sin Motion' (e.g., 'The ending of Inception is lazy writing')
A challenger can accept the debate within 48 hours
Community votes for a winner — voting open for 72 hours
Winner earns 'Verdict' badge on their profile
Debates are indexed and searchable by movie
A timeline discovery UI where users scroll horizontally through decades. Each era has visual styling matching its aesthetic — grain for the 70s, neon for the 80s, etc.
Each decade has a distinct CSS theme (visual filters, color palette, typography)
Top 12 films per decade displayed as poster grid
Click a poster to go to movie detail page
'Decade Mood' one-liner shown above each decade (e.g., 'America was scared. Hollywood pretended otherwise.')
Filter by genre within decade view
A curated editorial feed run by power users called Editors. Themed collections with real editorial voice, not algorithmic noise.
Editors role: apply via profile, approved by admin
Editors create Collections: title, tagline, 3–12 films, editorial note per film
Collections displayed as magazine-style cards in The Cut feed
Users follow Editors — collections appear in personalized Cut feed
id, imdb_id, tmdb_id, title, year, genre[], poster_url, plot, director, runtime, language
id, username, avatar_url, role (viewer / editor / admin)
id, user_id, movie_id, category, count, sin_note, created_at
id, movie_id, motion, challenger_id, defender_id, status, rounds[], votes, winner_id
Movie search, Sin Score, Director's Cut Journal, User auth, Mood Oracle
Cinematic DNA, The Void Engine, Decades Time Machine
Sin Debates, The Cut editorial feed, Editor roles
Algolia search, PWA, mobile polish, analytics, monetization
Project scaffolding: Next.js 14, Supabase, Tailwind, environment config
OMDb/TMDB API integration + movie search page
Movie detail page: poster, metadata, Sin Score widget
User auth: Supabase email + Google OAuth
Sin Score voting system with category breakdown
Director's Cut Journal: timestamp entry + public feed
User profile: watch history, sin votes, journals
Homepage: trending films, latest journals, Oracle CTA
Deploy to Vercel + connect custom domain
Primary palette: Deep navy (#1A1A2E), Crimson (#C0392B), Off-white (#F2F2F2)
Typography: Display — a slab serif or condensed grotesque. Body — clean sans-serif.
Motion: Page transitions feel like a projector change. Hover states are immediate, not sluggish.
Tone: Confident, dark-humored, cinephile-coded. Not corporate. Never cute.
Every screen must have a single dominant visual element (poster, DNA strip, debate motion)
Reduce cognitive load: each feature is discoverable within 2 clicks from homepage
Mobile-first: Oracle, Sin Score, and Journal all optimized for phone use
No modals for primary actions — navigate, don't interrupt
Dark mode default — this is a cinema app
OMDb API rate limits hit early
Implement Redis caching + TMDB as fallback
Seed with curated content; invite-first beta
Structured format limits bad faith; moderation queue
Rate limit votes; require email verification
Community correction mechanism built in from day 1
Monetization: Premium 'Sin Pass' subscription vs. advertising vs. Editor tipping?
Moderation: Async reviews — auto-flag or community flagging for Phase 1?
DNA seeding: Manual editorial tagging vs. AI-assisted genre/tone parsing for Phase 1?
Mobile app: PWA-first or native React Native in Phase 4?
Internationalisation: English-only for Phase 1; Spanish, French, Portuguese in Phase 2?

CineSins is architected to be built feature-by-feature using AI coding assistance. Each feature in Section 4 maps directly to a self-contained Next.js route + Supabase table + UI component set. Recommended AI build order:
Scaffold with create-next-app, install dependencies, set up Supabase
Build movie search + detail page (isolated, API-only dependency)
Build auth layer (Supabase, reusable across all features)
Build Sin Score widget (standalone component, Supabase integration)
Build Director's Cut Journal (most complex — build second-to-last in Phase 1)
Build Mood Oracle (pure frontend logic, no DB dependency initially)
Connect homepage with all Phase 1 features unified
Each feature has a dedicated /app/(feature) route folder. Keep features loosely coupled via Supabase — no feature should directly import another feature's components.
